name: Riley Sinema
title: Software/AI Engineer at Enzy
about: I'm a Software/AI Engineer at Enzy, where I build practical AI systems and solve challenging technical problems. I recently earned my Master's degree in Computer Science from Brigham Young University.

My background spans software engineering, machine learning, and data science. I've worked on everything from full-stack applications to deep learning research, and I enjoy projects that push me to learn new approaches and technologies.

My recent work has focused on building evaluation suites for agent systems and practicing evaluation-driven development for agents. I'm particularly interested in how we can make AI systems more reliable, measurable, and useful in real-world applications.

I love staying current with new research and technologies in the ML/AI space. Whether it's reading papers, experimenting with new tools, or diving into emerging frameworks, I'm always learning something new. My goal is to build AI systems that actually solve meaningful problems and make people's work easier.

projects.title: Deep Learning Calibration Research
projects.description: Developed a new calibration method for deep learning models that evaluates uncertainty on a per-instance basis. Published at ECAI 2025.

projects.title: Codenames AI Agent
projects.description: A creative agent that works as the Spymaster for the game Codenames. Uses a LLM and agentic flow to create a creative and effective clue for its team based on the current game state.

projects.title: Training a Bayesian LLM
projects.description: Using Bayesian methods to train LLMs that can quantify their uncertainty about their generated outputs.

projects.title: Creative Photography Assistant System
projects.description: A creative system that takes as input a picture that was taken by the user, evaluates the aesthetic value of the picture, finds transformations that improve the aesthetic quality of the image, and then makes suggestions to the user how they improve their photography skills to take more aesthetic pictures in the future.

projects.title: AWS Cloud Server Using Terraform
projects.description: Designing a cost-effective and secure cloud hosted game server. Architecture includes EC2 instance, proper network security configurations, Lambda function to start/stop/check status of server, etc., all set up and versioned using Terraform for Infrastructure as Code (IaC).

projects.title: LLM Creativity Metrics Comparison
projects.description: CS 501R final project comparing NoveltyBench's diversity metrics against simpler alternatives (pairwise embedding distance, n-gram diversity, BERTScore), correlated with human judgment. Generates k responses per prompt across multiple LLMs and scores them for both diversity and quality using an LLM-as-judge.

projects.title: Brick Mosaic Generator
projects.description: A self-hosted web app that turns an album's cover art into a buildable 1x1-plate brick mosaic, emitting a per-color parts list and printable build map for ordering. Features a configurable image pipeline with gamma-correct downsampling, dithering options, and ΔE2000 color matching.

projects.title: Social Network Community Detection
projects.description: Social network analysis of a university course interaction graph. Computes centrality metrics, detects communities with multiple algorithms (Louvain, Newman, spectral, and normalized Laplacian), performs one-mode projection, and simulates complex contagion across the network.

timeline.title: Teaching Assistant for CS 452 Concepts of Database Design
description: Assisting students in learning about database design

timeline.title: Master's in Computer Science
description: Conducted research in deep learning calibration, uncertainty estimation, and generative models
Built a robust enterprise-grade Text-to-SQL system that leverages large language models to convert natural language queries into optimized SQL commands for complex databases.
Relevant coursework: Natural Language Processing, Adv Software Engineering, Probabilistic Deep Learning, MLOps, Adv Deep Learning

Collaborated with senior engineers on architecture decisions for the refactor

timeline.title: Teaching Assistant for CS 452 Concepts of Database Design
description: Assisted students in learning about database design
Created interactive labs to help students learn database systems

timeline.title: Bachelor's in Computer Science

timeline.title: Software/AI Engineer
description: Developing LLM features with whole LLMOps lifecycle (data engineering, model fine-tuning, evaluation, deployment, monitoring)
Building evaluation suites for agent systems and practicing evaluation-driven development for agents
Architecting and designing machine learning and AI features for platform
